description: Applying a metadata filter after ANN search returns far fewer than k results.
body: Failure mode: the query fetches the top-k vectors first and filters by metadata afterward, but the nearest vectors were filtered out, leaving only a handful of matches even though many qualifying vectors exist further out. Detection signals: filtered queries return fewer than the requested k results, result count varies wildly with the filter, and the engine logs show filtering applied to the result set rather than the candidate set. Do not post-filter ANN results in a hot path. Safer fallback: use the engine's index-aware pre-filtering (payload filters, boolean expressions, or a partial index) so the predicate constrains the searched candidates, and verify a full k results return.
